tldr.bestFor: Sales outreach, training, and localization teams that want avatar-led videos with plan-based export limits
tldr.notFor: Light users who dislike monthly credit expiry or anyone expecting unlimited avatar generation
tldr.why: Free outputs are watermarked, credits expire after 30 days, and refunds are discretionary

miniTest.prompt: Create a 20-30 second talking-head video explaining a topic for an audience. Tone: professional. Include 1 hook, 3 key points, and a 1-sentence CTA

checklist.label: Export resolution
checklist.value: 720p / up to 1080p / up to 4K based on plan

checklist.label: Free-plan commercial use
checklist.value: FAQ says free-version videos may be used only for non-commercial purposes

checklist.label: Cancellation timing
checklist.value: Access continues through the current billing cycle after cancellation

checklist.label: Unlimited AvatarIV plan
checklist.value: No plan offers unlimited AvatarIV usage and credits

miniTest.verdict: Use this as a source-backed workflow check, not a completed hands-on run. HeyGen fits avatar-led outreach, training, and localization when export limits, credit expiry, and ownership rules match your use case.

useCases.title: Sales Outreach
useCases.why: Fits personalized avatar-led outreach when teams need quick presenter videos and can work within plan-based export limits

useCases.title: Training Videos
useCases.why: Works for onboarding and internal explainers where avatar delivery matters more than frame-level editing control

useCases.title: Localization
useCases.why: Useful for multilingual presenter-style videos when language coverage and plan limits fit the rollout

keyFeatures: Free plan outputs include a watermark
Export resolution depends on plan tier (Free/Creator/Team)
Subscription cancellation takes effect end of billing cycle
Refunds are granted only in specific cases, at discretion
Free plan use is non-commercial per FAQ
No plan offers unlimited AvatarIV usage and credits

pros: Free tier available for basic testing
cons: Credits expiring monthly can waste spend for light users
Refund policy is discretionary, not guaranteed
Unlimited marketing can confuse versus feature credit limits
User reports: consent verification can fail and block progress
Export format and system requirements need verification

verdict.bottomLine: HeyGen is best for teams who need avatar-led videos for sales outreach, training, and localization. The trade-off is cost control: credits expire after 30 days, and refunds are discretionary. Free outputs are watermarked; higher export resolutions and commercial rights depend on your plan tier.

faqs.question: Do free users get a watermark on exports?
faqs.answer: Yes—Free users get watermarked output on generated content. This is stated in official help documentation.

faqs.question: What export resolution do plans support?
faqs.answer: Official pricing states Free exports at 720p, Creator up to 1080p, and Team up to 4K. Higher resolution availability depends on your subscribed plan.

faqs.question: Do credits roll over to the next month?
faqs.answer: Credits expire 30 days after they are issued, so rollover beyond that window is not supported. Plan billing and issuance timing can still matter for how long you can use them.

faqs.question: Is there any plan with unlimited AvatarIV usage?
faqs.answer: No—official guidance says there is no plan, including enterprise, with unlimited AvatarIV usage and credits. You should expect feature usage to be credit-limited.

faqs.question: When does cancellation take effect after I cancel?
faqs.answer: Cancellation takes effect at the end of the current billing cycle. You typically keep access through the remainder of that cycle.

faqs.question: Can I get a refund if I forgot to cancel?
faqs.answer: Refunds are evaluated only under specific circumstances and at the company's discretion. User reports: this can clash with expectations when renewals happen.

faqs.question: Can I use Free plan videos commercially?
faqs.answer: The FAQ indicates Free plan usage is non-commercial. Commercial rights for paid plans: Needs verification from official terms or help.

faqs.question: What file format do exports download as (MP4, etc.)?
faqs.answer: Export/download documentation needs verification for explicit file format wording. Treat any assumed format as unconfirmed until verified.
